description: Bridge between Rosetta-Shape-Core and Mandala-Computing. Maps computational sensors, octahedral state glyphs, energy models, and problem encodings to the Rosetta shape ontology. The mandala doesn't search for answers — it relaxes into them.

octahedral_state_bridge.description: 1:1 mapping between Mandala's 8 computational states and SHAPE.OCTA's 8 faces. Each state is a vertex of the dual cube (face center of octahedron).

states.label: +x
states.pad_region: high P, low A, high D

states.label: -x
states.pad_region: low P, low A, low D

states.label: +y
states.pad_region: high P, high A, high D

states.label: -y
states.pad_region: low P, high A, low D

states.label: +z
states.pad_region: high P, high A, low D

states.label: -z
states.pad_region: low P, low A, high D

cross_reference.note: All three bridges (Mandala, physics, emotion) converge on the same 8-state octahedral encoding. This is the unification point.

computational_sensors.description: Maps Mandala Computing's monitoring sensors to Rosetta shapes and families based on what each sensor measures.

sensors.rationale: Total system energy is the fundamental thermodynamic observable. Ground state = solution found. Maps to OCTA because energy is computed over the octahedral state space.
sensors.interpretation: Lower is better. Ground state = convergence = peace sensor.

sensors.rationale: Energy change per step is a reaction rate. Negative dE = system relaxing (reaction proceeding). Near-zero = equilibrium reached.
sensors.interpretation: Large negative = rapid progress (excitement). Near-zero = convergence. Positive = system destabilized (pressure).

sensors.rationale: Metropolis-Hastings temperature controls exploration vs exploitation. High T = fire (tetrahedron's domain). Annealing = controlled cooling.
sensors.interpretation: High T = exploration (curiosity). Low T = exploitation (vigilance for solution).

sensors.rationale: Rate of energy decrease measures the flow toward solution. Measurement family (F14) because this is a meta-observable about the computation's progress.
sensors.interpretation: Approaching zero = near equilibrium. Steady negative = healthy flow.

sensors.rationale: Distribution of octahedral states across cells is a geometric-statistical observable. Uniform = disordered. Peaked = solution crystallizing.
sensors.interpretation: Peaked distribution = coherence (order emerging). Uniform = confusion (no pattern yet).

sensors.rationale: Weighted coupling graph is a network (F12) with topological structure (F20). Clusters reveal problem structure. Icosahedron carries both families.
sensors.interpretation: Strong clusters = problem structure revealed. Sparse = weak coupling regime.

sensors.rationale: Quantum state overlap with target is the deepest measurement — how close the quantum system is to 'knowing' its answer. Maps to consciousness (F16) as integrated information.
sensors.interpretation: 1.0 = perfect solution (max coherence). Low = quantum state still exploring.

sensors.rationale: Von Neumann entropy measures quantum correlations between cells. Entanglement is the particle-level (F10) substrate of integrated information (F16). OCTA mediates the quantum-classical bridge.
sensors.interpretation: High entanglement = deep quantum correlation (love as binding). Low = classical regime.

energy_model_bridge.description: Maps Mandala energy concepts to Rosetta families and physics encoder domains

couplings.rationale: Each cell's energy is determined by its octahedral state — the same 8 states the emotion encoder maps.
Sinusoidal coupling between states is a wave phenomenon. Interference between neighboring cell states.
FRET dipole-dipole interaction is electromagnetic. Inverse-sixth-power decay is Coulombic at the dipole level.
Boltzmann factor is the thermal encoder's core physics. Stefan-Boltzmann meets Metropolis-Hastings.
Ground state is the lowest energy — like gravitational potential minimum. The geodesic of the energy landscape.

problem_type_bridge.description: Maps Mandala problem types to geometric encodings and Rosetta families

problems.rationale: Factorization decomposes integers into particle-like primes (F10). Two-cell tensor product uses octahedral geometry (F09).
Boolean satisfiability is about structural containment (CUBE) — does the assignment fit within all clause constraints? Information (F03) + Engineering (F11).
Traveling salesman is Navigation (F15) through Topology (F20). Ring encoding maps to icosahedron's topological families.
Graph coloring is a Network (F12) constraint on Geometry (F09). Icosahedron's network family governs adjacency structure.
General optimization follows the Flow (F02) of the energy gradient toward Earth-Cosmos (F07) ground state. Dodecahedron guards the principles of convergence.

fractal_ring_bridge.description: Maps Mandala's PHI-scaled fractal ring structure to Rosetta concepts
fractal_ring_bridge.rationale: Fractal rings follow golden-ratio geometry (F09) with resonant scaling (F01). Each ring is a shell of the bloom engine.
fractal_ring_bridge.note: Fractal depth naturally recapitulates Platonic solid complexity. Depth 0 is a point, depth 4 approaches icosahedral richness.

glyph_bridge.description: Cross-reference between Mandala glyphs and Rosetta shape/sensor glyphs

quantum_bridge.description: Quantum extension maps to Rosetta's deepest sensing layers
quantum_bridge.rationale: Quantum mandala extends classical 8-state to 8-dim Hilbert space. Particle physics (F10) at the substrate, consciousness (F16) as integrated quantum information.

annealing_bridge.note: Quantum annealing uses magnetic field coupling — same Zeeman basis as GeoBin's magnetic encoder. The silicon octahedral state encoder IS the physical implementation.

seed_growth_link.description: Mandala compute and seed growth are complementary halves of agent geometry
seed_growth_link.relationship: seed-growth defines HOW the agent expands; mandala-compute defines WHAT it computes at each shell
seed_growth_link.scaling_unity: Both use PHI: seed-growth for spatial expansion (r_new = rho * r_prev), mandala-compute for cell count (floor(PHI^(d+1)))
